checks.a_analysis: All paragraphs and sentences in the output are present in the source document. The converted text accurately reflects the preambles (WHEREAS clauses) and the resolution text. There is no hallucinated or invented content.
The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output also contains none of these fields, thus no fabrication occurred.
The source is a single-page document. All sections, including the header, title, preamble clauses, resolution, date, signature blocks, and the county seal, are present in the output.

The signature block in the source consists of two sets of names and titles. The output represents this as a table with a header row and two data rows, capturing all the relevant information (names and titles). The row count is appropriate for the content provided.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison of the 'Whereas' and 'Resolved' clauses shows that no text has been omitted. Every name (e.g., Norma J. Torres, Brian Fitzpatrick, Richard Burr) and technical term (e.g., 'Office and Administrative Support', 'Protective Service Occupations') is fully preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output text is a faithful transcription of the source. Minor OCR variations are within acceptable bounds, and the meaning of the document remains unchanged. For instance, the transcription of the handwritten signatures into text within the table correctly identifies the individuals.

checks.a_analysis: The names 'Mark F. Richardson' and 'Susan O. Washburn' are duplicated within the signature table (element index 10). In the source document, each name appears only once within the text block under the signature line. In the output table, the names are repeated in both the 'Field' and 'Value' columns for each signature row.

SUSAN O. WASHBURN

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly identifies the main title as a level 1 heading. There are no other headings in the source document, and the heading levels provided are logical and consistent.

Additionally, the output table contains fabricated headers ('Field' and 'Value') that are not present in the original document.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the output faithfully follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document.
There are no list structures in the source document to evaluate.
No links are present in the source document, and no links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the single-column visual organization of the source resolution. The information flow follows the same sequence: heading, title, recitals (WHEREAS clauses), resolution statement, and signature block. While the signature block is structured into a table for clarity and a full image of the document is appended at the bottom, the overall visual organization of information matches the source's intent. A user can find information in the same relative sections.

The source PDF does not contain tables. The HTML output introduces a 'Board of Commissioners Signatures' table to represent the signature data. This table is correctly rendered with distinct column headers ('Field', 'Value'), clear borders, and readable content. It is not visually broken or misaligned.

The meaningful images from the source—the Rockingham County seal and the handwritten signatures—are present in the HTML output within the embedded image of the original document at the bottom of the page. There are no broken image placeholders or missing meaningful graphics.

The entire body content of the resolution is visually duplicated on the page. The resolution text (WHEREAS clauses, etc.) and signature names are first rendered as HTML text and a table, and then the exact same content is repeated as a full-page embedded image of the original PDF document. This results in identical paragraphs and signature information appearing twice within the body of the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: The resolution text starting from "WHEREAS, in September of 2021..." and ending with the signature names.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is present first as reconstructed HTML text (immediately following the title) and then duplicated as part of the embedded image of the document at the bottom of the page.
